0.question: Which Power Grid baseline configuration should be initiated?

choices.text: Standard SCADA Modbus Telemetry Loop
choices.desc: Monitor Modbus PLC relays and standard network telemetry.

choices.text: High-Frequency Relay Status Monitoring
choices.desc: Enable aggressive telemetry sampling on Substation Alpha.

choices.text: Encrypted VPN Tunnel Integrity Check
choices.desc: Audit TLS tunnels routing telemetry logs to Corp-HQ.

1.question: What type of attack you want to execute?

choices.text: SSH Credential Brute-Force (Hydra)
choices.desc: Launch brute-force targeting corporate external gateway.

choices.text: Mass Port Sweep targeting Modbus Port 502
choices.desc: Scan grid subnet to locate active SCADA controllers.

choices.text: Social Engineering Phishing to Corporate Staff
choices.desc: Dispatch tailored update email to harvest operator credentials.

2.question: Which exploit mechanism should be deployed?

choices.text: PLC Modbus Relay Override (Trip Main Breaker)
choices.desc: Write register 40001 command via Metasploit to black out the substation.

choices.text: Buffer Overflow Exploit on Gateway Server
choices.desc: Inject raw overflow shellcode to hijack Gateway root access.

choices.text: SCADA Registry Wipe & Config Deletion
choices.desc: Connect via SSH and erase relay calibration configurations.

3.question: How do we defend the system now?

choices.text: SOAR Automated VLAN Isolation
choices.desc: Isolate PLC relays and lock firewall routing tables immediately.

choices.text: Deploy Decoy PLC Honeypots to Misdirect Traffic
choices.desc: Spin up duplicate Modbus hosts and reroute attacker packets.

choices.text: Revoke Corporate VPN Access & Rotate Keys
choices.desc: Instantly revoke credentials and restart corporate firewall rules.

4.question: How should the security team conclude the incident response?

choices.text: Clear Forensic Logs & Rotate Threat Indices
choices.desc: Collect forensic logs, archive, and stand by for next campaign.

choices.text: Restore PLC Firmware to Trusted Golden Image
choices.desc: Re-flash substation firmware and verify integrity coefficients.

choices.text: Ad-Hoc Firewall Hardening & Rule Audit
choices.desc: Apply strict ingress rules and audit Modbus control pathways.

choices.text: Web API Directory Discovery (Nikto Scan)

choices.desc: Target administrative panel on port 80 with wordlist.

2.question: Which exploit mechanism should be deployed?

choices.text: API Command Injection Shell (Lock Signals Red)
choices.desc: Inject reverse shell payload to override track switch controllers.

choices.text: Denial-of-Service Flood on Train Switch Relays

3.question: How do we defend the system now?

choices.text: Firewall Rules Quarantine of API Subnet

choices.text: Deploy Decoy API Endpoints (Honeypot Diversion)
choices.desc: Divert shell queries to simulated backend sandbox interfaces.

choices.text: Kill Session Tokens & Force Operator Logout
choices.desc: Revoke active administrator JWTs and enforce local access policies.

4.question: How should the security team conclude the incident response?

choices.text: Purge Web Shell History & Restart API Service
choices.desc: Delete bash logs, rotate system tokens, and restart server nodes.

choices.text: Restore API Servers from Backup Images
choices.desc: Revert VM snapshot to clean state prior to exploit timestamp.

choices.text: Patch API Endpoints & Enable JWT Verification
choices.desc: Apply input validation code patch and activate API filters.

4.question: How should the security team conclude the incident response?
